page: Amazon Web Services m8gn.12xlarge is a general-purpose, network-optimized server powered by the AWS Graviton processor family. It features 48 dedicated physical cores on the ARM64 architecture using the ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 design running at 2.8 GHz, with 1.0 thread per core. The memory configuration consists of 192.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio. The instance operates on the Nitro hypervisor, lacks local storage, and includes a baseline network bandwidth of 150 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core capabilities and exceptional memory performance. The server achieves top-tier results in large-block memory bandwidth reads at 435.08 GB/sec and writes at 216.67 GB/sec, as well as in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and software compilation. Single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench text processing scoring in the average range. However, multi-core Geekbench and PassMark database operations both rank in the top tier, demonstrating high resource density and parallel processing efficiency.

Given its high network bandwidth and balanced memory-to-CPU ratio, the m8gn.12xlarge is highly cost-efficient for network-bound and memory-intensive workloads that do not require local NVMe storage. It is well-suited for static web serving, where it achieves top-tier throughput, as well as database operations, redis caching, and software compilation. It also shows strong performance in text generation for medium and large language models, though it is less efficient for small-model text generation.
description: Amazon Web Services m8gn.12xlarge is a general-purpose, network-optimized server powered by 48 dedicated ARM64 physical cores using the AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 architecture. Operating at 2.8 GHz on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 192.0 GB of RAM (4.0 GB per core), 150 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth, and no local storage. Benchmarks show top-tier performance in large-block memory bandwidth, multi-threaded decompression, and software compilation, alongside strong multi-core processing. Single-core performance remains average. This resource profile makes the instance highly efficient for network-heavy workloads, database operations, redis caching, and static web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8gn.12xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose server with 48 dedicated Graviton cores, 192 GB RAM, and 150 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for database and web serving workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8gn.12xlarge is a 48-core ARM64 server with 192 GB RAM and 150 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for high-throughput workloads.
tagline: An ARM64 general-purpose server offering high-bandwidth networking, dedicated physical cores, and top-tier memory throughput for parallel workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated ARM64 physical cores using AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2
192.0 GB of RAM with top-tier large-block memory bandwidth
150 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput networking
Top-tier performance in software compilation and database operations
Optimized for static web serving, redis caching, and multi-threaded decompression
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
